explanations.6: The first number (8080) is the port on your computer; the second number (80) is the port inside the container that the server listens on.
explanations.8: The 'depends_on' section tells Docker to start the db service first, but it won't wait for it to be ready unless you add the 'condition' check below it.
explanations.9: This makes Docker Compose wait until the database actually responds to health checks before starting the server, preventing connection errors on startup.
explanations.12: Setting 'restart: always' means Docker will automatically restart this database container if it crashes or stops for any reason.
explanations.15: Instead of putting passwords directly in the file, 'secrets' lets you reference sensitive data stored separately and pass it to the container securely.
explanations.17: This creates a named volume (a persistent storage area) and mounts it into the container so database files survive even if the container is deleted.
explanations.20: Rather than storing the password as plain text in environment variables, this tells PostgreSQL to read the password from a file that Docker injected securely.
explanations.23: The healthcheck runs a command periodically to verify the database is actually ready to accept connections, not just that the container started.
